records.ety: [OF. oppugner, L. oppugnare; ob (see Ob-) + pugnare to fight. See Impugn.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Oppugned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Oppugning.]

defs: To fight against; to attack; to be in conflict with; to oppose; to resist.
quotes: They said the manner of their impeachment they could not but conceive did oppugn the rights of Parliament.
